hero.role: Senior fullstack engineer · 5+ years · Remote from Brazil
hero.sub: I help companies turn ideas into clean, fast and reliable products — from the first sketch to the version that ships and grows. Equally comfortable shaping the architecture, writing the code, and talking to the people who use it.

bigNumbers.title: Six years of building things that don't break.

stacks.label: Languages and frameworks in daily use

countries.label: Countries with clients I've worked with

about.title: A builder who sweats the details and ships the work.
bio: I'm Marco — most people online know me as maarkn. I'm a senior fullstack engineer based in Goiânia, Brazil, working with clients and teams around the world.

I started writing software professionally in 2019 and never really stopped. Over the years I moved from frontend trainee to leading the architecture of products used by thousands — VoIP platforms, fintech apps, real-estate assistants, museum sites, agronomy chatbots, and a few things I can't talk about yet.

What I really enjoy is being involved end-to-end: understanding what people actually need, designing how the system should behave, writing the code that makes it real, and staying close while it goes live and grows. I care about clean architecture, but I care more about software that works, doesn't surprise the team that maintains it, and gives the user a smooth ride.
When I'm not coding, I'm usually studying — currently improving my English to relocate abroad, and exploring how AI agents can be useful day-to-day instead of just impressive in demos.

values.title: End-to-end ownership
values.body: From the first conversation to the version running in production. I don't hand things off and disappear.

values.title: Clear, plain communication
values.body: I translate between business and code. Stakeholders get straight answers, not jargon.

values.title: Pragmatic over perfect
values.body: I ship the simplest thing that solves the problem, then iterate when reality pushes back.

values.title: Respect for the next person
values.body: Code is read more than it's written. I leave the codebase a little better than I found it.

freelance.summary: Building end-to-end products as a freelancer: a refactored Flutter app with in-app purchases, several WhatsApp assistants powered by LLMs, a carbon-credit reporting platform, a real-estate chatbot for Miami, and a few security and performance audits.

nectar.summary: Led the architecture of a VoIP product integrated with the company CRM. Designed billing rules, real-time provisioning, call recording and transcription, multitenancy and team management.

sevencred.summary: Built a fintech API for FCO loans with a microservices mindset, a Flutter app for applications, a Ghost-based portal, and crawlers ingesting data from public Brazilian gov APIs.

ecto.summary: Migrated Google reviews ingestion from batch to real-time events. Led two junior developers, set the code-review process, and shipped a subscription system to onboard small clients.

vendorhub.summary: Drove UI standardization, refactored the core product registration flow, and built a Chrome extension to analyze Mercado Livre ad performance directly inside the marketplace.

ecto1.summary: Shipped features on a Google My Business listings platform, managed deployments, and improved a few Gatsby blogs along the way.

collectgram1.summary: Built referral and subscription flows, fixed bugs and grew the admin dashboard of a collection-management product.

collectgram0.summary: Where it all started: a Vue.js marketplace dashboard and email marketing templates with Pug and Sass.

toolkit.title: The tools I reach for, day after day.
toolkit.sub: These are the languages, frameworks and ideas I use most. The list isn't exhaustive — I learn whatever the project needs — but it's an honest snapshot of where I'm fluent.

projects.title: Things I've shipped that people actually use.
projects.sub: A small slice of the products and platforms I've built over the last few years — for clients, employers and a couple of side bets. Some I can show in detail, others stay anonymous behind an NDA.

taglines.miami-real-estate-assistant: A WhatsApp assistant for a Miami real-estate agency that captures leads, syncs properties from MLS and answers questions like a tireless junior agent.
taglines.municipalities-chatbot: City-by-city WhatsApp helper that crawls each town's website, indexes it, and answers citizens' questions about services, opening hours and documents.
taglines.voip-platform: Calls, recordings, transcriptions and dynamic billing in a single VoIP product wired into the company's CRM.
taglines.fintech-loan-api: A loan platform for a Brazilian agribusiness fund: APIs, a Flutter app for applicants, and crawlers ingesting public government data.
taglines.carbon-credit-platform: Triage chatbot plus an automated report engine that turns raw project data into legal-style viability documents.
taglines.drug-leaflet-platform: A high-performance, SEO-first reference for medication leaflets — Astro on the front, NestJS and ANVISA crawling on the back.
taglines.museum-institutional-site: Institutional site for a museum, with a Ghost-based CRM, SEO-driven content and interactive webframes for in-museum kiosks.
taglines.vanilla-farming-assistant: An agronomic assistant for vanilla growers, grounded in vectorized technical documentation.

miami-real-estate-assistant.description: A Miami-based real-estate agency wanted a way to capture and qualify leads at the speed of WhatsApp. We built an assistant that talks to prospects in natural language, syncs the catalog of properties from the local MLS feed and an internal admin, and walks visitors through neighborhood, price range and bedroom count without ever feeling like a form.
miami-real-estate-assistant.role: Architecture, backend, MCP integration with the MLS marketplace, lead/property/agent admin dashboard, web chat SDK and an LLM-driven ETL that imports new developments from PDFs, Word and Excel files.
features: Lead capture and routing via WhatsApp with handoff to a human agent
MLS sync and ongoing property enrichment with LLM-based descriptions
Admin dashboard for leads, properties and agent profiles
Embeddable web chat SDK for partner sites

municipalities-chatbot.description: A platform that lets any city plug a WhatsApp assistant into its citizen services in days. The agent crawls the city's website, indexes the content into a vector store, and answers questions about garbage collection, opening hours, paperwork and more in plain language.
municipalities-chatbot.role: Crawler design, vector indexing, retrieval pipeline, WhatsApp integration, multitenant configuration so each city has its own knowledge base and tone of voice.
features: Per-city crawl and vector index that refresh on a schedule
Retrieval-augmented answers grounded in official municipal content
Multitenant admin to manage cities, fallback messages and brand voice
WhatsApp Business API integration with conversation analytics

voip-platform.description: A new vertical for a Brazilian CRM company: a VoIP product that doesn't feel bolted on. Calls, recordings, transcriptions and dynamic billing live next to the customer record, so a sales rep can pick up where the last conversation ended.
voip-platform.role: Backend lead — designed the architecture, built billing rules, real-time provisioning of extensions and DIDs, audio optimization, multitenancy and team management.
features: Internal and national calls with recording and transcription
Dynamic billing rules per team and tenant
Real-time provisioning of extensions and DIDs
Multitenancy, role-based features and usage analytics
Audio optimization to reduce bandwidth without losing quality

fintech-loan-api.description: A loan platform for a Brazilian agribusiness fund. We built the backend that powers the application flow, the Flutter app the borrower uses, and the data pipelines that pull and normalize information from a handful of public government APIs.
fintech-loan-api.role: Backend architecture in microservices, Flutter app for borrowers, ETL of public data (SIGEF, RFB) into MongoDB, code review and mentoring of a junior developer.
features: Microservices designed for fintech-grade scalability and security
Flutter app for loan applications and customer onboarding
Crawlers and ETL pipelines that normalize public government data
Public-facing portal built with Ghost CMS and Handlebars

carbon-credit-platform.description: A platform that helps carbon-credit project owners triage their projects and produce viability reports. The triage flow lives in a chatbot; the reports come out of an automated engine that turns project data into a legal-style document the team can edit and send.
carbon-credit-platform.role: Backend, prompt engineering and report templating engine. Worked with the founders to translate domain knowledge into structured, reproducible outputs.
features: FAQ and triage chatbot grounded in project documentation
Automated viability report generation with legal-style formatting
Editable templates so the operations team can refine outputs
Document export ready for client delivery

drug-leaflet-platform.description: A reference site for medication leaflets in Brazil, built for speed and SEO. The frontend is Astro for fast static delivery; the backend is NestJS with a crawler that ingests data from ANVISA so leaflets stay up to date.
drug-leaflet-platform.role: Backend in NestJS, ANVISA crawler, content normalization, Astro frontend for high-performance SEO pages.
features: Astro-based frontend delivering very fast, SEO-first pages
NestJS backend serving the leaflet API
ANVISA crawler that keeps the catalog fresh
Schema.org markup ready for search-engine ingestion

museum-institutional-site.description: Institutional website for a museum, with a Ghost-based CMS the curatorial team can run on its own. The site doubles as the back-end of interactive webframes used in the museum's physical kiosks.
museum-institutional-site.role: Architecture, Ghost configuration and Handlebars theming, kiosk webframe development, SEO setup.
features: Headless-friendly Ghost CMS for curators and staff
Kiosk webframes for in-museum interactive experiences
SEO and content strategy for visibility outside the building
Brand-aligned theme built with Handlebars

vanilla-farming-assistant.description: A specialist assistant for vanilla growers, grounded in vectorized agronomic documentation. Producers ask questions in plain language and get answers that come from real technical material instead of a generic model.
vanilla-farming-assistant.role: Document ingestion pipeline, vector store setup, prompt engineering, WhatsApp delivery channel.
features: Vectorized library of agronomic documents specific to vanilla cultivation
Retrieval-augmented answers with citations to source material
WhatsApp distribution so producers don't need to install anything
Feedback loop that surfaces gaps in the underlying documentation

page.title: Every product I'm willing to put my name on.
page.sub: All projects on a single page, filtered by category. The list is hand-curated — small bets, big platforms, and a few things I'm proudest of.

miami-real-estate-assistant.shot3: Admin dashboard for agents and inventory

projects.privateNote: Source code is private — happy to walk a recruiter or hiring panel through it on a call.

contact.title: Have something you'd like to build? Let's talk.
contact.sub: Quickest path is the form below — I read every message and reply within a couple of working days. If you prefer a different channel, pick whatever fits.

form.name: Your name
form.successBody: Thanks for writing. I'll reply from markimkr@gmail.com within a couple of working days. If something is urgent, ping me on WhatsApp or LinkedIn.
form.errorInvalidEmail: That doesn't look like a valid email
form.errorTooLong: A bit too long — please shorten it
form.errorSendFailed: Couldn't send the message right now. Try the email link above instead.

links.name: Marco Filho
links.bio: Senior fullstack engineer working remotely from Brazil. Building products that ship, not slides that don't.

chat.title: Ask anything about Marco's work.
chat.sub: I'm a small assistant trained on Marco's resume, projects and writing. I'll do my best to give straight answers — and tell you when I don't know.

panel.title: maarkn assistant
panel.subtitle: Ask anything · powered by AI
panel.empty: Hey! I'm the maarkn.dev assistant. Ask me about Marco's projects, stack, availability or how to reach him.
panel.rateLimited: You've reached the rate limit for this hour. Try again later — or email markimkr@gmail.com.
panel.errored: Something went wrong. Try sending the message again.
suggestions: Tell me about the Miami project
panel.poweredBy: AI replies are generated and may contain mistakes. For anything important, contact Marco directly.

blog.title: Field notes from someone who ships.
blog.sub: Short essays on building products, working with AI, architecture choices, and the discipline of getting things into people's hands. New posts when there's something worth writing down.
blog.empty: No posts yet. Check back soon.

self-employed-freelance.tagline: Building products end-to-end for clients across LatAm and the US.
self-employed-freelance.context: After ten years in product engineering inside companies, I turned to consulting: same craftsmanship, different shape of accountability. Each engagement is a thin contract focused on shipping the smallest thing that earns the next conversation.
responsibilities: Discovery, scoping and proposal: translate a fuzzy brief into a one-page plan and an honest estimate.
Architecture and tech choice: pick the boring stack that fits the size of the team and the speed they want.
Hands-on coding across stacks (TypeScript, NestJS, Next.js, React, Flutter) and infra (Docker, AWS, GCP).
Working alongside founders and product folks during launch and the first weeks in production.
achievements: Shipped a Miami real-estate WhatsApp assistant that captures and qualifies leads from the first message.
Designed and built a multitenant municipalities chatbot used by city halls in Brazil.
Refactored a Flutter app from Express to NestJS without breaking the live App Store / Play Store releases.
Built an automated viability-report engine for a carbon-credit platform.
self-employed-freelance.outcome: Six paid clients, every retainer renewed, and zero rollback-grade incidents in production.

nectar-crm.tagline: Led the architecture of Nectar's first VoIP product, integrated end-to-end with the company CRM.
nectar-crm.context: Nectar is one of Brazil's mid-market CRM players. They wanted a VoIP that didn't feel bolted on. I joined as Senior L2 to lead the product side of that vertical from architecture down to bandwidth optimization.
responsibilities: Designed the multitenant architecture, the billing rules engine and the real-time provisioning flow.
Owned the implementation of call recording, transcription and usage analytics.
Tuned the audio codec negotiation to reduce bandwidth costs by ~30% without losing call quality.
Partnered with product and ops on operator workflows, role-based features and onboarding.
achievements: Shipped internal and national calling, recording and transcription as a first-class CRM feature.
Stabilized real-time provisioning of extensions and DIDs through idempotent state transitions.
Reduced incident frequency by rebuilding the cache layer with strict tenant scoping.
nectar-crm.outcome: VoIP became a sellable add-on for existing CRM customers and a new acquisition channel.

sevencred.tagline: Built the backend, the mobile front and the data pipelines for an FCO loan platform.
sevencred.context: Sevencred was a fintech building a loan platform on top of FCO (a Brazilian agribusiness fund). Small team, big regulatory surface. I took ownership of the backend and the borrower-facing mobile app.
responsibilities: Designed APIs in TypeScript + NestJS as microservices ready for fintech-grade scale and security.
Built a Flutter app for loan applications and customer onboarding.
Implemented crawlers and ETL pipelines that ingested public government data (SIGEF, RFB) into MongoDB.
Mentored a junior developer through code review and pair programming.
achievements: Shipped a complete onboarding flow that went from public APIs to a normalized profile in seconds.
Stood up a Ghost-based public portal for SEO and content marketing.
Held the technical end of the early-stage product alongside the founders.
sevencred.outcome: Project paused pre-MVP after a funding shortfall, but the codebase was demoable and the team transitioned cleanly.

ecto-digital-senior.tagline: Migrated Google reviews ingestion from batch to real-time and shipped a self-serve subscription system.
ecto-digital-senior.context: Ecto Digital builds tools for Brazilian local businesses on top of the Google My Business API. I was promoted from fullstack to senior, then took the lead on a few cross-cutting initiatives.
responsibilities: Architected the migration from batch routines to a real-time event-driven pipeline for review ingestion.
Introduced a code-review process and mentored two junior developers.
Built a self-serve subscription system to onboard small clients without a sales call.
Owned production deployments and on-call response for the systems I shipped.
achievements: Cut review-to-dashboard latency from ~hourly to seconds.
Self-serve subscription brought in a long tail of small businesses that previously couldn't be reached.
Lifted code-review coverage to 100% of merged changes within two months.
ecto-digital-senior.outcome: The pipeline I designed continued to run after I left, and the subscription system became the default onboarding path.

vendorhub-sistemas.tagline: Drove UI standardization on a marketplace integration platform and built a Mercado Livre Chrome extension.
vendorhub-sistemas.context: VendorHub aggregates product catalogs from Mercado Livre, Magalu, Shopee and B2W into one back office. The frontend had grown organically and needed a tighter visual system.
responsibilities: Standardized core UI patterns across the product registration flow.
Refactored the variations form so non-technical sellers could keep their listings consistent.
Co-implemented backend feature enhancements alongside the platform team.
Set up commit linting and a code-review process.
achievements: Released a Chrome extension that analyzes Mercado Livre ad performance directly inside the marketplace UI — used by sellers to tune their listings without leaving the site.
vendorhub-sistemas.outcome: The Chrome extension stayed alive after my departure and the variations refactor halved the volume of seller support tickets in that area.

ecto-digital-fullstack.tagline: Owned features, deployments and a couple of legacy Gatsby blogs at Ecto Digital's earlier stage.
ecto-digital-fullstack.context: First role at Ecto, before the senior promotion. Wide remit on a small team — I was equally likely to be debugging a Gatsby build, deploying to production or wiring up a new GMB-backed feature.
responsibilities: Implemented features on top of the Google My Business API for listings management.
Managed deployments and rollback procedures for the small fleet.
Maintained a couple of Gatsby-based blogs and tightened their SEO setup.
achievements: Shipped enough sustained value to be promoted to Senior Fullstack Developer.
ecto-digital-fullstack.outcome: Solid foundation across the team's stack and the customer success motion.

collectgram-jr.tagline: Grew the admin dashboard for a collection-management product and shipped the referral funnel.
collectgram-jr.context: Collectgram is a SaaS for collectors (cards, coins, the like). After the trainee year I moved to the JR slot and started taking ownership of multi-week features end-to-end.
responsibilities: Built the referral feature with tracking and reward logic.
Designed the subscription CTA section that became the default upsell.
Fixed bugs across the admin dashboard and improved its overall responsiveness.
Maintained Vue.js + Vuex codebase and SCSS design system.
achievements: The referral feature drove a measurable lift in monthly signups during its first quarter live.
collectgram-jr.outcome: Closed out the year ready for a senior-track move into a larger team.

collectgram-trainee.tagline: Where it started — a marketplace admin in Vue.js and email marketing templates with Pug and Sass.
collectgram-trainee.context: First professional role. The team trusted me with real production tickets from week one — I am still grateful for it.
responsibilities: Implemented features across the Vue.js 2 marketplace admin dashboard.
Built email marketing templates with Pug and Sass on a Node.js pipeline.
Paired with senior engineers on code reviews and feature scoping.
achievements: Earned the JR promotion at the end of the trainee period.
collectgram-trainee.outcome: The first set of habits — tests, code review, communication — were formed here.
